_comment: v1.4.1 + v1.5 — scripted LLM responses for the cockpit e2e specs. The fixture is bind-mounted into portal-csr via docker-compose.e2e.yml and selected by BSS_LLM_FIXTURE_PATH. orchestrator/bss_orchestrator/llm_mock.py owns the read + match logic. Match strings are case-insensitive substring searches against the latest user message. Each fixture entry's `steps` walks one astream_once invocation (= one cockpit turn); compound actions that span multiple /confirm rounds need ONE entry per turn — the second entry typically matches the synthetic '(operator typed /confirm — ...)' rewrite the cockpit sends on a /confirm-bracketed turn.

responses.name: tool-roundtrip-customer-list
responses._comment: Spec test_cockpit_tool_roundtrip — exercises the LLM → tool → render loop. customer.list against a name_contains filter is the cheapest read tool that returns a stable rendering, and it doesn't require a known customer_id (the e2e spec just needs SOMETHING to come back; the seeded demo customers cover this when make demo-restore has run).

responses.name: propose-then-confirm-destructive
responses._comment: Spec test_cockpit_propose_then_confirm — verifies the destructive-tool gate proposes (doesn't execute) until /confirm fires. provisioning.set_fault_injection is in DESTRUCTIVE_TOOLS but has the cheapest blast radius (single config row in a sim service). The args target a non-existent service so /confirm executes against the real provisioning-sim and returns an error — but the spec only asserts the proposal UI appeared and was consumed by /confirm, not the tool's success.

responses.name: knowledge-uncited-fallback
responses._comment: Spec test_cockpit_knowledge_citation_or_fallback — the cockpit's hallucination guard (_RE_KNOWLEDGE_CLAIM) replaces uncited handbook claims with the canonical fallback string. Mock returns a single content message with the trigger phrase 'per the handbook' and NO knowledge.* tool call. The guard fires; the spec asserts the fallback text is present.

steps.content: Per the handbook, refunds are issued at the operator's discretion within 30 days of purchase.

responses.name: v15-compound-action-turn1-propose-first-destructive
responses._comment: Spec test_compound_action_granular / test_compound_action_batched — turn 1 of a compound action. Operator's prompt 'plan compound' triggers the LLM to propose TWO destructive tools in the same step (LangGraph emits both as tool_calls in one AIMessage). The wrapper blocks the FIRST one regardless of mode because allow_destructive=False on the operator's initial prompt. The cockpit stages the first as pending_destructive and waits for /confirm. We pick provisioning.set_fault_injection (cheap blast radius, no real-state precondition required) so the spec can run repeatedly without dependent fixtures.

steps.content: Proposed the first destructive step. Type /confirm to authorise.

responses.name: v15-compound-action-turn2-after-confirm-tries-two-destructives
responses._comment: Spec test_compound_action_granular / test_compound_action_batched — turn 2 after /confirm. Cockpit rewrites the user message to '(operator typed /confirm — proceed with the prior destructive proposal now; call the tool)'. The mock matches that substring and tries to call BOTH destructive tools in a row. Granular mode: the wrapper executes the first and re-blocks the second, so the cockpit stages a NEW pending_destructive for the second. Batched mode: the wrapper executes both and no new pending row gets staged. The specs assert on the cockpit.pending_destructive row count via asyncpg to differentiate.

steps.content: Compound action complete (or second step pending /confirm under granular mode).

responses.name: v15-case-investigation-chained-reads
responses._comment: Spec test_case_investigation — operator prompt 'investigate' triggers a chain of THREE read tools in one astream_once turn. Tests that the v0.19 'Done.' rule is softened (the agent can call read N, then read N+1, then read N+2, then terminate with a one-sentence summary). All three tools are read-only so the autonomy mode doesn't matter here. The case.list call is the cheapest discovery read; customer.list and catalog.list_active_offerings flesh out the chain. The final content asserts that the agent terminates with a brief summary instead of duplicating tool data as markdown.

steps.content: Investigation complete — see the three cards above for the case roster, customer roster, and active offerings.
